Green tea is made from Camellia sinensis. Rich in antioxidants and catechins.
Black tea is fully oxidized. Varieties: Assam, Darjeeling, Earl Grey.
Oolong tea is partially oxidized with a floral flavor and aids digestion.
Matcha is powdered Japanese green tea with L-theanine for calm focus.
White tea is the least processed, made from young buds. Sweet and delicate.
Chamomile tea helps with sleep and anxiety. Caffeine-free herbal tisane.
Peppermint tea aids digestion and relieves bloating with a cooling effect.
Hibiscus tea is tart, deep red, and high in Vitamin C.
Ginger tea helps with nausea and inflammation. Made from fresh ginger root.
The global tea market combined revenue (at-home and out-of-home) reached approximately $292 billion in 2025.
At-home tea revenue worldwide amounts to around $143 billion in 2025, sold through supermarkets and convenience stores.
Out-of-home tea revenue worldwide — from restaurants, cafes, and hotels — amounts to around $148 billion in 2025.
The at-home tea market is expected to grow at a CAGR of 5.63% from 2025 to 2030.
Black tea leads the global tea market with roughly 39% of global tea revenue in 2024.
Green tea is the second most popular category worldwide, reaching about $15 billion in 2024.
China is by far the largest tea market in the world. It generates around $51 billion in at-home tea revenue in 2025 — about five times the Indian market.
China is the world's leading tea producer, producing over 3.18 million metric tons in 2023 — nearly 49% of global production.
India is the second largest tea producer with 1.365 million metric tons produced in 2023.
Other major tea-producing countries include Kenya (353,000 tonnes), Sri Lanka (251,000 tonnes), Turkey (246,000 tonnes), and Vietnam (174,000 tonnes).
Global tea production stands at 6.6 million metric tons in 2023, up 26% over the past decade.
Only about 26% of global tea production is exported; the rest is consumed domestically.
The United States is the biggest importer of tea worldwide, importing over $519 million worth in 2023.
Other major tea importers by value include Pakistan ($634M), Russia ($412M), UK, Iran, UAE, Egypt, Iraq, and Morocco.
Global tea consumption amounts to approximately 31 million tons in 2024.
People around the world drink about 6 billion cups of tea every day, making it the most consumed hot drink globally.
Turkey has the highest per capita tea consumption — approximately 1,300 cups per person per year.
The Asia-Pacific region leads global tea consumption, accounting for about 34.7% of worldwide market share in 2024.
The hot drinks market overall reached $371 billion in 2024 and is expected to grow 7.4% yearly from 2025 to 2030.
Tea holds a 39% revenue share in the global hot drinks market in 2024.
The average volume of tea consumed per person at home worldwide is estimated at 0.93 kg in 2025.
The global tea market volume at home is expected to reach 7.33 billion kg in 2025.
Unilever (Lipton), Associated British Foods (Twinings), and Tata Global Beverages (Tetley) are the most important global tea companies by revenue.
The rise of online shopping is making specialty and premium teas more accessible to consumers worldwide.
Organic and specialty teas are among the fastest-growing segments, driving the overall market growth of 6.6% CAGR from 2021 to 2028.
Ready-to-drink (RTD) tea products are growing rapidly, driven by consumers seeking convenience combined with health benefits.
Green tea contains roughly 90% polyphenols, 7% amino acids, and 3% caffeine. Its catechins may help prevent cancer, control cholesterol, and support weight loss.
Green tea captured 13.6% of the American tea market in 2024 due to its well-known health benefits.
The US combined tea market (at-home and out-of-home) is estimated at $25.1 billion in 2024.
Black tea accounts for about 66% of global tea imports by volume, mostly in packs over 3 kg.
The global tea market is forecast to reach $152 billion in wholesale value by 2035, growing at a CAGR of 2.1% from 2024.
الشاي الأخضر غني بمضادات الأكسدة ويدعم صحة القلب والأيض.
الشاي الأسود مؤكسد بالكامل. أشهر أنواعه: أسام، دارجيلينج، إيرل غراي.
شاي الأولونج مؤكسد جزئياً بنكهة زهرية ويساعد على الهضم.
شاي الماتشا مسحوق الشاي الأخضر الياباني. يعزز التركيز الهادئ.
الشاي الأبيض أقل أنواع الشاي معالجة. طعمه حلو وغني بمضادات الأكسدة.
شاي البابونج يساعد على النوم ويقلل التوتر. خالٍ من الكافيين.
شاي الكركديه أحمر اللون وحامض الطعم وغني بفيتامين C.
طريقة تحضير الشاي الأخضر: ماء 70-80 درجة، انقع 2-3 دقائق.
طريقة تحضير الشاي الأسود: ماء مغلٍ 100 درجة، انقع 3-5 دقائق.
حجم سوق الشاي العالمي بلغ نحو 292 مليار دولار في 2025 مجمعاً بين المنزل وخارجه.
الصين هي أكبر سوق للشاي في العالم بإيرادات منزلية تبلغ نحو 51 مليار دولار في 2025.
الصين تنتج 49% من إنتاج الشاي العالمي بأكثر من 3.18 مليون طن متري في 2023.
الهند ثاني أكبر منتج للشاي بـ 1.365 مليون طن في 2023.
تركيا تتصدر استهلاك الشاي للفرد عالمياً بنحو 1300 كوب للشخص سنوياً.
الشاي الأسود يمثل نحو 39% من إيرادات سوق الشاي العالمي في 2024 ويتصدر جميع الأنواع.
الشاي الأخضر يحتل المرتبة الثانية عالمياً بحجم سوق حوالي 15 مليار دولار في 2024.
منطقة آسيا والمحيط الهادئ تقود استهلاك الشاي عالمياً بحصة 34.7% من السوق العالمي في 2024.
يُشرب نحو 6 مليارات كوب شاي يومياً حول العالم، مما يجعله المشروب الساخن الأكثر استهلاكاً.
الولايات المتحدة هي أكبر مستورد للشاي في العالم بأكثر من 519 مليون دولار في 2023.
مصر من بين أكبر مستوردي الشاي في العالم وتُصنَّف ضمن أعلى الدول استهلاكاً للشاي الأسود.
الشاي العضوي وشاي المتخصصين من أسرع القطاعات نمواً في سوق الشاي العالمي.
الشاي الجاهز للشرب (RTD) يشهد نمواً سريعاً بسبب إقبال المستهلكين على الراحة مع الفائدة الصحية.
إنتاج الشاي العالمي بلغ 6.6 مليون طن متري في 2023، بزيادة 26% خلال العقد الماضي.
